tagline: The score is a fact. The story gets checked.
MONETIZATION_RULE: STANDING RULE (strategy desk, 2026-07-21): NO monetization of any kind (ads, AdSense, affiliate links) on any page fed by MLB StatsAPI, NHL api-web, or ESPN endpoints (scoreboards, content API) until a formal terms review of those feeds is completed. Publishing unmonetized content from them continues as-is (acceptable use). Monetizing pages built on these feeds without that review is the quiet terms violation that gets API access cut. Any session wiring ads or affiliates MUST check this rule first. The per-feed terms flags live in sources.league_apis.note.

budget.note: Hard fail-closed cap restored to the Haiku economy sizing (cost lockdown 2026-07-21, crypto parity); a typical full run lands ~$0.15-0.30. The top_n 12 volume setting adds only cents at Haiku prices.

researcher.note: Role 1 of the three-role editorial pipeline: builds the structured research brief (data points + confidence labels + the other side of the story) from the fetched source pages. Fact fidelity is the whole job. COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21): Haiku, crypto-desk parity.

writer.note: Full-length stories (350-650 words) in batches of 3 per call; the cap covers 3 complete drafts with headroom so JSON never truncates. COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21): Haiku, crypto-desk parity.

approver.note: Role 3: post-draft last line of defense (accuracy/liability -> balance -> quality, categorized rejections). A different call from the writer so the builder never approves their own work.

models.note: COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21, supersedes the quality-launch A/B): crypto is the priority bet and this desk must never run a higher cost than the crypto desk. claude-haiku-4-5 on every desk stage, exactly like crypto; the deterministic fail-closed gates (source liveness, dedup, depth gate, belts, corroboration rules) are the quality floor, not the model. wraprescue keeps claude-sonnet-5 for the rare edition-failure path only, same as crypto. REVERSAL TRIGGER: real audience traction on this desk -> revisit the writer model.

publicist.note: Publicist DISABLED per strategy 2026-07-21: LinkedIn is currently the government-sales channel. Code kept in the repo, schedule removed from publicist.yml. Re-enable only on explicit direction.

wrap.note: The Daily Edition (Morning Brief / Closing Wrap, wrap.py): synthesis over the desk's own published stories. COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21): Haiku, crypto-desk parity.

wrapcheck.note: Independent trace check on the edition: every specific fact must come from the provided inputs; no betting advice or predictions; calm register. Separate call from the writer.

aging.note: Monthly post-publication review (aging.py, report-only): has any published story's premise been invalidated? Conservative flagging; a human decides on corrections.

wraprescue.note: The Daily Edition's second-chance generator: when the Haiku edition fails its trace check, the corrective rewrite runs here.

sources.note: source_tier drives editorial weight. primary = official league/institutional sources (trust most: box scores, transactions, discipline rulings straight from the league). major = established outlets with editorial standards. mixed = aggregated or rumor-adjacent feeds (never publish alone; two-source rule applies). All feeds below are free and keyless, verified live 2026-07-19. The reliability layer is the pipeline (tiering, two-source corroboration, adversarial verifier, primary-source linking), not any single API.

rss.name: ESPN NFL
rss.fallback_note: espn.com RSS serves HTTP 202 bot challenges to datacenter IPs (GitHub runners); the API tier serves everyone. aggregate.espn_api_fallback maps this endpoint when RSS fails.

rss.name: ESPN NBA
rss.fallback_note: espn.com RSS serves HTTP 202 bot challenges to datacenter IPs (GitHub runners); the API tier serves everyone. aggregate.espn_api_fallback maps this endpoint when RSS fails.

rss.name: ESPN MLB
rss.fallback_note: espn.com RSS serves HTTP 202 bot challenges to datacenter IPs (GitHub runners); the API tier serves everyone. aggregate.espn_api_fallback maps this endpoint when RSS fails.

rss.name: ESPN NHL
rss.fallback_note: espn.com RSS serves HTTP 202 bot challenges to datacenter IPs (GitHub runners); the API tier serves everyone. aggregate.espn_api_fallback maps this endpoint when RSS fails.

sources.keywords_note: Optional per-feed 'keywords' gate: high-volume aggregated feeds keep only items matching a keyword in headline+snippet, so they cannot flood the editor. Items matching a 'narratives' watchlist entry always pass the gate.

league_apis.note: Official and quasi-official structured data endpoints, all keyless and verified live 2026-07-19. These are the desk's PRIMARY factual sources: the researcher and verifier treat league-published schedules, scores, and transactions as ground truth that outranks any outlet's retelling. balldontlie was dropped (now key-gated, 401). Upgrade path if the desk ever needs deeper stats: API-Sports (~$10/mo) or SportsDataIO, deliberately excluded from v1 under the free-only constraint. TERMS FLAGS: MLB StatsAPI is public but historically non-commercial terms; ESPN scoreboard/content endpoints are unofficial with no stated terms (revocable); NHL api-web is undocumented. See MONETIZATION_RULE at the top of this file: no ads or affiliate monetization on pages fed by these until a formal terms review.

endpoints.name: ESPN Scoreboard NFL
endpoints.note: Unofficial-but-stable ESPN JSON. Reliable data, no SLA; a fetch failure is a documented skip, never a pipeline failure.

endpoints.name: TheSportsDB
endpoints.note: Community-maintained, free test key. Lookup/reference only, never a sole source.

narratives.note: The desk's ongoing-storyline watchlist, maintained by the editor-in-chief. A cluster whose headline/snippet matches any keyword gets tagged with the narrative's name in items.json, the editor treats a genuine development in a tagged narrative as presumptively rank-worthy, and watchlist matches always pass a feed's keyword gate. Seeded for July 2026; add narratives as they emerge, retire them when they resolve.

dedupe.note: Near-identical stories across outlets (same event, many rewrites) collapse into one cluster by normalized-headline token overlap or shared URL. The cluster keeps the highest-tier source as primary and lists the rest as corroboration.

publish.not_financial_advice: GoCheckMySports reports events. It never advises bets. Nothing here is betting or gambling advice.

newsletter.note: Owned audience, highest value, build first. Set endpoint + key to enable.

video.note: Newscast script handoff to the recording workflow.
